all_violations.description: Latency budget check uses Date.now() wall-clock elapsed time, which can vary under CI host load and create non-deterministic failures near threshold.
all_violations.suggestion: Keep the latency assertion but add bounded slack or capture service timing metrics for a more stable threshold gate.
all_violations.code_snippet: const start = Date.now(); ... const elapsedMs = Date.now() - start; expect(elapsedMs).toBeLessThanOrEqual(storyE5Context.latencyBudgetMs);

all_violations.description: Tests create mappings and receipt rows but do not perform explicit teardown, relying on uniqueness and retention windows for isolation.
all_violations.suggestion: Add optional test-scope cleanup hooks (or reset helpers) for long-running shared environments to keep state bounded.
all_violations.code_snippet: const mappingResponse = await apiRequest(request, { method: 'POST', path: storyE5Context.paths.numbersCollection, ... });

all_violations.description: Factory defaults use stable tenant/orgUnit identifiers; this is practical but can increase cross-run coupling in persistent shared test environments.
all_violations.suggestion: Allow easy override to per-run tenant/orgUnit in environments where data reset is not guaranteed.

all_violations.description: Spec file is 320 lines, exceeding preferred maintainability threshold and making scenario-level changes harder to review safely.
all_violations.suggestion: Split by behavior slice (dedupe identity, retention controls, burst latency) while preserving shared fixture imports.

all_violations.description: Spec file is 336 lines and contains multiple repeated setup sections that increase maintenance overhead.
all_violations.suggestion: Extract repeated request/setup blocks into helper functions or parameterized test builders.
all_violations.code_snippet: // 336-line API automation spec containing three expanded scenarios

all_violations.description: Number mapping setup and signed webhook dispatch patterns are repeated across tests with minor differences.
all_violations.suggestion: Factor shared arrange steps into helper utilities to reduce duplication and improve readability.
all_violations.code_snippet: const mappingResponse = await apiRequest(...); const firstResponse = await apiRequest(...);

all_violations.description: Both E2E ATDD scenarios are currently skipped, leaving operator/UI acceptance behavior unexecuted in active suites.
all_violations.suggestion: Unskip once UI contracts are available and include these in PR smoke gates.

all_violations.description: Positive-path replay and retention behavior is well covered, but explicit invalid signature/auth rejection scenarios are not present in this reviewed set.
all_violations.suggestion: Add negative tests for bad signature headers and unauthorized admin retention endpoints.

all_violations.description: Retention cleanup tests validate success and dry-run behavior but do not assert explicit failure/error response paths.
all_violations.suggestion: Add failure-path assertions for invalid policy windows and backend cleanup faults.

all_violations.description: A hard latency threshold based on process wall-clock can be sensitive to host noise in shared CI runners.
all_violations.suggestion: Use percentile/service-timing-based budget checks or add controlled tolerance for CI jitter.

all_violations.description: Each scenario re-creates mapping/setup independently; this is safe but increases suite runtime and boilerplate.
all_violations.suggestion: Use scoped reusable setup helpers where safe to reduce repeated overhead.

high_severity_violations.description: Spec file is 320 lines, exceeding preferred maintainability threshold and making scenario-level changes harder to review safely.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Split by behavior slice (dedupe identity, retention controls, burst latency) while preserving shared fixture imports.

high_severity_violations.description: Spec file is 336 lines and contains multiple repeated setup sections that increase maintenance overhead.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Extract repeated request/setup blocks into helper functions or parameterized test builders.
high_severity_violations.code_snippet: // 336-line API automation spec containing three expanded scenarios

high_severity_violations.description: Both E2E ATDD scenarios are currently skipped, leaving operator/UI acceptance behavior unexecuted in active suites.
high_severity_violations.suggestion: Unskip once UI contracts are available and include these in PR smoke gates.

top_10_recommendations.recommendation: Use a stable latency assertion strategy for CI-host variance (bounded slack or service-timing metric assertions)
Retain deterministic provider event IDs and signed header generation approach already present
Keep avoiding hard waits and conditional control flow in test bodies
Introduce optional teardown/reset utility for mappings/receipts
Keep fixture-based context generation to preserve test independence
Preserve unique deterministic event/message IDs to avoid collisions
Split long API specs into focused files by AC/risk domain
Retain current test ID and priority naming discipline across new files
Promote E2E ATDD cases from skipped to active once UI contracts are ready
